title: AI-based UK law firm reports its first court trial win
friendlyTitle: An AI-powered UK law firm helped win a real court case
summary: Computer Weekly reports that Garfield AI, a regulated AI-based UK law firm, supported a freelancer through a Wandsworth County Court case that recovered £7,000 in unpaid fees. Garfield AI helped with correspondence, proceedings, document production, witness statements, trial bundles, and counterclaim response, while both sides used barristers at trial.
friendlySummary: A freelancer used Garfield AI to help recover £7,000 in unpaid fees. The AI service helped prepare legal documents and court materials.
whyItMatters: The practical signal is access to justice for smaller claims where traditional legal costs can exceed the value of the dispute. Regulated AI legal services may first reshape routine, document-heavy cases before they affect complex litigation.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows how AI may make smaller legal claims more affordable. It is not magic-lawyer-in-a-box, but it can help with expensive paperwork.
